activities.name: Louvre Museum
activities.description: World's largest art museum. Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, Winged Victory. Book timed entry online.

activities.name: Eiffel Tower Visit
activities.description: Iconic iron tower (1889). Summit access via elevator. Book tickets 2 months ahead.

activities.name: Montmartre Food Walk
activities.description: Crêpes at Le Consulat, cheese at La Fermette, wine tasting. Sacré-Cœur views.

activities.name: Seine River Cruise
activities.description: See Notre-Dame, Louvre, and Musée d'Orsay from the water. Especially beautiful at sunset.

activities.name: Musée d'Orsay
activities.description: Impressionist masterpieces — Monet, Renoir, Van Gogh, Degas. Housed in a stunning Beaux-Arts train station.

activities.name: Le Marais Neighborhood Walk
activities.description: Historic Jewish quarter, Place des Vosges, falafel on Rue des Rosiers, boutique shopping.

activities.name: Maison Européene de la Photographie
activities.description: Important center for contemporary photography with a large exposition area having three or four major expositions per year dedicated to contemporary photography. Housed in an updated hôtel particulier built in 1706. Ther

activities.name: Better Paris Photos
activities.description: Better Paris Photos offers instructional tours and workshops that combine hands-on learning of essential photographic techniques with guiding to, and commentary about, the most photogenic spots of Paris. Led by English-s

activities.name: La Tour Eiffel
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: l'Assemblée Nationale
activities.description: Guided tours conducted all day, ID required. Formerly the Palais Bourbon, this building has housed the National Assembly, the French parliament's lower house, since 1827. Some interesting architecture and the library fea

activities.name: Hôtel des Invalides
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Musée de l'Armée
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Musée du quai Branly
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Musée Rodin
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Musée des Égouts de Paris
activities.description: see in Paris
activities.location: Entrance opposite 93, quai d'Orsay near the Pont d'Alma, {{station

activities.name: Vedettes de Paris
activities.description: Discover Paris by river on a chic boat.

activities.name: Champagne on the Eiffel Tower
activities.description: Enjoy a glass of champagne at the top of the Eiffel Tower and finish your visit on a festive note. The Champagne Bar, located on the top floor of the tower, is both elegant and original. The bar, which is part of the bui

activities.name: Le Bon Marché
activities.description: One of the world's first department stores, the oldest and one of the largest in modern Paris.

activities.name: Magasin Sennelier
activities.description: In 1887 Gustave Sennelier founded this house next to the Ecole des Beaux Arts.

activities.name: Rue Cler
activities.description: Market street 10 minutes from the Eiffel Tower. The market is at its best on Tu–Sa, 08:30–13:00 or 15:00–19:30; Su, 08:30–12:00; closed on M.

activities.name: Chez Germaine
activities.description: It's all about home-cooking, like your grandma would do if she were French and trained in cooking.

activities.name: Au Pied de Fouet
activities.description: It's an appropriate name for a restaurant renowned for its unapologetically rude wait staff. Some people say that this is part of the charm of the place, perhaps that fits your definition of the word "charm" as well? Or

activities.name: Chez l'Ami Jean
activities.description: A sensational little restaurant featuring food and wine from the Basque region.

activities.name: Le Clos des Gourmets
activities.description: A great little restaurant, elegant without being stuffy, popular with foreign visitors and American diplomatic staff. French cuisine with a twist. Try the avocado millefeuille with orange sauce for dessert, a surprisingl

activities.name: Tribeca
activities.description: A very nice terrasse on the rue Cler (pedestrian area). The food is simple, but good and reasonably cheap.

activities.name: 58 Tour Eiffel
activities.description: The restaurant in the first floor of the Eiffel Tower. One pays of course for the view, not so much for the food.

activities.name: Gâteaux Thoumieux
activities.description: Chef patisser Ludovic Chaussard, who had previously worked with Alain Ducasse is designing the cakes.

activities.name: Le Petit Tibéro
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Le Voltaire
activities.description: Le Voltaire has always been tres chic and with customers like Helene Rochas and the Rothschild family, you know you are dining with the right set. Peaceful wood paneling and lighting that flatters, it is an intimate and

activities.name: Avenue des Champs-Elysées
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Place de la Concorde
activities.description: The largest square in Paris with fantastic vistas in every direction. It was in this square (then called la Place de la Revolution) that the French King Louis XVI, Marie Antoinette and many others were guillotined during
activities.location: At the east end of the Champs-Elysées

activities.name: l'Arc de Triomphe
activities.description: see in Paris
activities.location: Place de l'Étoile, Place Charles de Gaulle

activities.name: Église de la Madeleine
activities.description: One of the best-known and most beautiful churches in Paris, in the guise of a Corinthian order classical temple. Construction started in 1764, although the church was not finally consecrated until 1845. The Madeleine has

activities.name: Élysée Palace
activities.description: The official residence of the President of France since 1848. Dating to the early 18th century, it contains the office of the President and the meeting place of the Council of Ministers. The name Élysée derives from Elys

activities.name: Pont Alexandre III
activities.description: The bridge is widely regarded as the most ornate, extravagant bridge in the city. It is classified as a French Monument historique.

activities.name: Le Grand Palais
activities.description: Built in 1900 for the universal exposition, the Grand Palais was an engineering feat and a milestone of design, marking the transition between historicism and modern architecture. It remain impressive today due to its un

activities.name: Musée du Petit Palais
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Palais de la decouverte
activities.description: In a magnificent the western wing of the Grand Palais, this museum forms a part of science-themed education-oriented universcience establishment together with the La Cite des Sciences in [[Paris/19th arrondissement

activities.name: Jerome de Noirmont
activities.description: This museum represents some key contemporary and emerging artists such as Jean Pierre Raynaud, Eva and Adele, and Jeff Koons.

activities.name: Musée Cernuschi
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Musée Jacquemart-André
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Parc Monceau
activities.description: One of Paris's best kept secrets, painted by Monet numerous times. It is a very elegant centre of green in the middle of cosmopolitan Paris. Make sure you check out the statues, entrance rotunda and the surrounding mansi

activities.name: Lido de Paris
activities.description: The most famous cabaret celebrates spectacle revues. Children ages 4 and older are welcome. Casual elegant dress code. Coat and tie appreciated. Shorts, athletic clothing and tennis shoes are not allowed.

activities.name: Carre Marigny
activities.description: The open-air market for trading postage stamps and other similar collectibles

activities.name: C42
activities.description: C42 is the flasghip Citroen store occupying an entire building, designed by Manuelle Gautrand and completed in 2007, being the first new building in the Avenue in some 30 years. The unmistakable facade is inspired by Cit

activities.name: Atelier Renault
activities.description: Renault has opened its Champs-Elysees showroom at this location in 1910. The highlight of its current form, launched in 2011, is the cafe/restaurant/bar in the "floating" mezzanine, where you can enjoy some good drinks a

activities.name: Guerlain
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Avenue Peugeot
activities.description: While not as spectacular from the outside as its sister brand Citroen's building, the Avenue Peugeot is also worth a visit if you are a car fan, as it always has a temporary exhibition of some of Peugeot's concept or com

activities.name: Louis Vuitton Building
activities.description: An unmistakable piece of modernist architecture at the corner of Avenue George V, the Louis Vuitton Building was erected in 1913 and in the 21st century continues to serve as the flagship for the luxury brand, housing th

activities.name: Christian Dior
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Louis Vuitton
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Valentino
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Chanel
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Montaigne Market
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Zadig & Voltaire
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Hermes flagship store
activities.description: Hermes has been occupying those premises since 1880, contributing to the street's long-lasting reputation as the hub for luxury apparel and accessories.

activities.name: Lanvin flagship store
activities.description: Jeanne Lanvin moved her maison de couture to premises facing Hermes in 1889, where it remained to this day.

activities.name: Chez Francis
activities.description: View of the Eiffel Tower.

activities.name: Diep
activities.description: Thai, Chinese, and Indonesian. Vegetarian friendly.

activities.name: Kokohana
activities.description: Two chefs battle against each other in a spectacular performance of chopping, slicing, sauteing everything from scallops to foie gras. The food is average, but the presentation is well worth it.

activities.name: Villa Spicy
activities.description: Warm and peaceful close to the Champs-Élysées serving an organic detox menu.

activities.name: Hanawa
activities.description: Great sushi in a nice atmosphere, extensive menu.

activities.name: Ladurée
activities.description: Famous for their macaroon cookies, which come in over 15 different flavours. Expensive, but an experience. There is a tea room, a bar, and a restaurant. You can also order baked goods to go, in fancy boxes and bags.

activities.name: Le Marais
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Place des Vosges
activities.description: The oldest planned square in Paris, and what many tourists as well as locals consider the heart of the Marais area.

activities.name: Rue des Rosiers
activities.description: The Jewish quarter, with many small delis and shops. Lively on most days except for Saturdays.

activities.name: Île Saint-Louis
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Notre-Dame de Paris
activities.description: see in Paris
activities.location: Ile de la Cité 6, Place du Parvis Notre Dame

activities.name: Hôtel de Ville
activities.description: Many feel that this, Paris' town hall, is one of the loveliest buildings in the city. You might not get that from the front view, but try watching the light change on its roofs and towers during sunset from one of the ca
activities.location: Place de la Hôtel de Ville

activities.name: Tour St Jacques
activities.description: A [[Gothic architecture

activities.name: La Bastille
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Mémorial de la Shoah
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Centre Georges Pompidou
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Maison de Victor Hugo
activities.description: The house in which the famous French novelist Victor Hugo once lived.

activities.name: Hôtel de Sully
activities.description: Built in 1625, the Hotel de Sully is an interesting house with some sculptures in a beautiful courtyard. The house features special exhibitions, so check listings when in Paris.

activities.name: Marché aux fleurs Reine-Elizabeth-II
activities.description: On the north side of the Ile de la Cité, the main island at the centre of Paris you'll find a burgeoning daily flower market, where you can buy just about any type of flower, and oddly enough, a range of exotic tropical

activities.name: Opéra BD
activities.description: Comics (Bandes Dessinées) are a serious art form in France and even regular corporate bookstores have a good selection, but it's much better to look for them where they are really appreciated, in one of Paris's many priv

activities.name: Mariage Frères
activities.description: If you love tea, you'll love this old salon de the in the Marais.

activities.name: BHV
activities.description: Is a general store with most of everything: clothes, perfumes, furniture, you name it—it's especially famous for its basement section, dedicated to handiwork & DIY --think of it as a small Home Depot in a basement, thoug

activities.name: Chez Marianne
activities.description: At the corner of rue des Hospitalières-Saint Gervais, and the rue Rosiers, Chez Marianne—like the many falafel stands in the Pletzle—serves excellent sandwiches out of a walk up window, but unlike some of the others also

activities.name: La Perla
activities.description: Good Mexican food to be had within a stone's throw of the Ile de St. Louis. They mix a decent margarita too!

activities.name: Flunch
activities.description: Self-service restaurant, right next to the Pompidou centre. Steaks etc. are cooked fresh when ordered, other food is pre-cooked and there is a microwave in the table area should you need to heat anything up. A chance to

activities.name: O Corcovado Marais
activities.description: This Brazilian restaurant serves authentic food.

activities.name: Creperie Beaubourg
activities.description: The restaurant serves salty "galette" and sweet "crepes" with cider for a reasonable price. In summertime one can sit nicely outdoor.

activities.name: Le Loir dans la Théière
activities.description: A highly recommended and quaint little tea shop, well worth a visit especially for brunch, which at €15.50 is a great value for the quantity, quality, and ambience. The cakes are huge, but if one is too late they might a

activities.name: Pain Vin Fromages
activities.description: It's all about cheese, with a selection of Swiss dishes, and others.

activities.name: Le Colimaçon
activities.description: Cozy, charming space. Attentive service. French cuisine with daily specials.

activities.name: L'Ambroisie
activities.description: In the aristocratic décors of the 18th century the chef Bernard Pacaud and his son prepare modern and refined food. The "tarte au chocolat" is surreal.

activities.name: Brasserie Bofinger
activities.description: A beautiful, historical décor in the Art Nouveau style (see the ceiling upstairs); good Alsatian cuisine : choucroute, kuglopf, etc, plus good classic cuisine de brasserie.

activities.name: Le Georges
activities.description: On the top floor of the Centre Georges Pompidou, the restaurant Georges offers a panoramic view of Paris. Serves modern French food with classic desserts like "blancs en neige".

activities.name: Amorino
activities.description: The oldest of the many boutiques operated by this gelato (Italian ice cream) maker.

activities.name: Glacier Berthillon
activities.description: The most renowned French sorbet has his little tea shop since 1954. The wait in the queue is definitely worth it, also with some luck the queue isn't that big. On the corner, the queue for takeaway is usually bigger but,

activities.name: Pozetto
activities.description: Little shop that's both a café and sells Italian ice cream. The ice cream flavours vary according to the season.

activities.name: Sacré-Cœur
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Cimetière de Montmartre
activities.description: There are a number of famous occupants, but the real reason to visit this cemetery is to see the ornate tombstones, sculptures, and other sometimes macabre, sometimes touching memorials Parisians have left here for their

activities.name: Artists' Square
activities.description: Numerous artists paint portraits of tourists and also sell their paintings.

activities.name: Place Pigalle
activities.description: The sleaze of boulevard de Clichy between place Pigalle and place Blanche can provide a moment of distraction. Be warned if you are male it is better to do this in the company of a female fellow traveller, as the clubs o
activities.location: On the border with the [[Paris/9th arrondissement

activities.name: Moulin de la Galette
activities.description: Built in 1717, the windmill is now part of a restaurant of the same name, and is easily visible from the street.

activities.name: Moulin Blute-Fin
activities.description: The windmill is within fenced private property, and may be difficult to see from the street, especially if there are leaves on the nearby trees. It is near the more visible Moulin de la Galette.

activities.name: Espace Dali
activities.description: A fantastic and undeservedly little-known collection of the great surrealist artist's often overlooked sculptural works. Those travellers seeking a more authentic experience should be warned that the majority of works he

activities.name: Musée de Montmartre
activities.description: The museum is located in a 17th century house with a garden, and features the history and culture of Montmartre. Several famous artists had lived in the house including Renoir.

activities.name: "I love you" wall
activities.description: Wall with the words for "I love you" written in many languages.

activities.name: Abbesses métro station
activities.description: The station's beautiful entrance (circa 1900) is one of only two remaining glass-covered "dragonfly" entrances designed by Hector Guimard (1867–1942). It was featured in the film Le Fabuleux destin d'Amélie Poulain. Whil

activities.name: Villa Léandre
activities.description: Villa Léandre is a short residential street where the houses have small front gardens giving the street an English look.

activities.name: Vigne du Clos Montmartre
activities.description: Montmartre has a small vineyard which can be seen through a chain-linked fence. One of its products (vin Clos Montmartre) can be purchased at the nearby Musée de Montmartre.

activities.name: Buste de Dalida
activities.description: Dalida was a chanteuse and actrice who sold 170 million albums and singles worldwide. The bust faces the attractive Rue de l'Abreuvoir. The breast area of her bronze bust has a bleached colour possibly due attention give

activities.name: Le Passe-Muraille
activities.description: The sculpture of a man walking through a store wall like a ghost was inspired by a novel by Marcel Aymé about a civil servant who takes revenge on people who humiliated him after he discovered he could pass through walls

activities.name: Le Moulin Rouge
activities.description: With two shows a night, this turn-of-the-20th century burlesque palace offers a big production choreographed dance show interspersed with comedians, jugglers, and magicians. The show is more than bilingual, actually play

activities.name: Cooking with Class
activities.description: Hands on French cooking classes in a relaxed atmosphere with an experienced French chef. 4 hours of fun, including market visit, cheese and wine tasting, 5 minutes from the Sacre Coeur in the heart of Montmartre.

activities.name: Môm'artre
activities.description: An arts centre intended primarily for kids (Môme means kid) the Môm'artre features workshops for kids and adults, hosted by neighborhood artists.

activities.name: Marché aux Puces de St-Ouen
activities.description: Widely-acclaimed as the largest flea market in all of , in existence since 1885, this sprawling bazaar is made up of both permanent stalls and temporary stands (over 2,000 of them), arranged in winding, sometimes chaotic

activities.name: Ouistitipop
activities.description: New and used kids' clothing, toys, strollers and furniture. They take consignments too.

activities.name: Thibault van der Straete
activities.description: Clothing boutique featuring designs made of the rare and luxurious alpaca wool. His soft and sumptuous men's and women's designs are a favourite of those enchanted by ethno-chic.

activities.name: Spree
activities.description: Hip and trendy bazaar that features designs and clothes from all around the world. Its large space also hold artist exhibitions.

activities.name: Au Marché de la Butte
activities.description: The grocery store featured in the film Le Fabuleux destin d'Amélie Poulain still functions as such but also displays memorabilia from the film in its windows, and sells some as souvenirs.

activities.name: Nawab
activities.description: This Indian and Pakistani restaurant is usually packed especially for lunch so be sure to call ahead and reserve a table. One of the reasons is that if you ask them to spice it up "like in Pakistan" they actually will.

activities.name: Le Surcouf
activities.description: Very ordinary non-touristy cafe run by family from the Maghreb. "French" food is pricey for what you get, but the couscous dishes are huge, cheap and delicious with a home-cooked feel to them. There's a dog hanging aroun

activities.name: La Travesia
activities.description: Inexpensive but excellent Lebanese food. Vegetarian friendly. Just down the steps from the Sacre Coeur.

activities.name: Au Grain de Folie
activities.description: A one-woman operation with some mixed reviews, but apparently when she gets it right it's just about the best dining experience you'll ever have. Booking a day ahead is suggested.

activities.name: Le Buffet
activities.description: Tasty, homey French food in a family-run restaurant. Lunch menu is around €12 (can be had until 20ː00).

activities.name: Cafe des Deux Moulins
activities.description: A popular destination for those who love the film Le fabuleaux destin d'Amelie Poulain, yet some locals still come here. Interior preserves the movie set. Great for lunch.

activities.name: La Petaudiere
activities.description: Piano bar. Tasty food, nice ambience, and excellent good piano music (prepare some coins for the pianist).

activities.name: La Taverne de Montmartre
activities.description: Small restaurant with a nice rustic decoration, just below the artist's square, place du Tertre. Relaxing place, not overloaded with tourists. Excellent fondue offered for €17 per person (Oct 2008), including a salami sa

activities.name: Markotte
activities.description: Organic sandwiches, salads and soups
activities.location: At the St Ouen Flea market

activities.name: Le Basilic
activities.description: Very nice atmosphere and service. Great food, and good selection of wines.

activities.name: Le Refuge des Fondues
activities.description: Fondue restaurant for young people: menu for €17, including wine served in a baby bottle, appetizers, the fondue itself (cheese or meat), and dessert.

activities.name: Musée Grévin
activities.description: The Musée Grévin is a waxwork museum with some 500 characters arranged in scenes from the history of France and modern life.

activities.name: Musée Gustave Moreau
activities.description: The house of the symbolist painter has now become a museum to his life and work.

activities.name: Opéra Garnier
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Colorful basketball court
activities.description: An unusually colorful court in the middle of buildings.

activities.name: les Grands Boulevards
activities.description: Go shopping at the grands magasins (department stores).

activities.name: Olympia
activities.description: A music hall, which houses concerts for an older crowd. Be sure to tip the usher €2 or so.

activities.name: Théâtre Édouard VII
activities.description: French theatre, for those with command of the language.

activities.name: Galeries Lafayette
activities.description: Founded in 1894, and is the flagship store of the department store chain. The gourmet section is a Paris institution that takes up an entire floor, featuring (among many other things) several competing vendors of deluxe

activities.name: Printemps
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Bouillon Chartier
activities.description: Since 1896. Menu changes regularly. Historic building and interior. Classically dressed servers. Satisfying, cheap food. Traditional, representative French cooking; don't expect innovative, haute cuisine. Usually the lin

activities.name: La Marmite
activities.description: Nice sunny place for having lunch.

activities.name: L'Opéra Restaurant
activities.description: A modern restaurant designed by Odile Decq opened in the 19th century Opéra Garnier and convenient to Galeries Lafayette or Printemps.

activities.name: Pizzeria Da Carmine
activities.description: Good (real) Italian restaurant/pizzeria. Does not accept credit cards.

activities.name: Pizza Marzano
activities.description: UK travellers will be familiar with the above-average pizza / pasta menu, speedy table service and very reasonable prices. This outlet has a large al fresco area for the warmer months.

activities.name: Le Roi du Pot-au-Feu
activities.description: The menu is very short, but the meals are big and good. The best pot-au-feu in the world. Very nice, typical Parisian atmosphere.

activities.name: Juste
activities.description: Super fresh, simply served and cheap seafood by a fish monger / restaurateur. Takeout, Outdoor Seating. Arrive at noon to avoid lines. Review the menu on-line before arriving to order quickly at the counter. Raw oysters,

activities.name: Les Catacombes de Paris
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Cimetière de Montparnasse
activities.description: The burial place of existentialist philosopher Jean-Paul Sartre, feminist Simone de Beauvoir (both of whom lived nearby); musician Serge Gainsbourg; artist Man Ray; the poets Charles Baudelaire, Guy de Maupassant, Charle

activities.name: Cité Internationale Universitaire de Paris
activities.description: The C.I.U.P. is a student quarter established in 1925, providing homes for about 10,000 students, scientists, and artists from 120 countries. It has 40 houses attributed to individual nations. The individual houses organ

activities.name: Fondation Henri-Cartier-Bresson
activities.description: The foundation opened its doors in May 2003. It preserves Henri Cartier-Bresson and Martine Franck’s archives. Exhibition of photographs, paintings, sculptings, and illustrations.

activities.name: Lion of Belfort
activities.description: This is a bronze replica in smaller scale of a monument created by Bartholdi, the sculptor of the Statue of Liberty, in order to commemorate Denfert-Rochereau, who defended the city of Belfort in the Franco-Prussian war

activities.name: Observatoire
activities.description: The observatory was erected by Claude Perrault from 1667 to 1672 by order of Colbert. The building is oriented to the four cardinal directions. It is crossed in the centre by the Paris meridian (2° 20′ 14″ east of Greenw
activities.location: 61, av de l'Observatoire, entrance: 77, av Denfert-Rochereau

activities.name: Parc Montsouris
activities.description: Considered one of the most colourful Parisian parks, a local favourite, the Parc Montsouris was opened in 1878, having been designed by Alphand for Baron Haussmann. A large man-made lake and waterfalls are surrounded by

activities.name: Port-Royal Abbey
activities.description: Famous people who stayed here include Marie de Rohan, intriguer during the Fronde; Jeanne Baptiste d'Albert de Luynes, future mistress of a duke of Savoy; Marie Angélique de Fontanges, mistress of Louis XIV, died here gi

activities.name: Barrière d’Enfer
activities.description: The two pavilions were built in 1784 to 1787 by the French architect Claude-Nicolas Ledoux, who erected many similar toll houses at the entrances to the city.

activities.name: Cartier Foundation for Contemporary Art
activities.description: A museum of contemporary art. It was founded in 1984 by the well-known jewel and watch manufacturer, Cartier. In 1994 it moved to the new building erected on plans of the architect Jean Nouvel.

activities.name: The Liberation of Paris, General Leclerc Museum, Jean Moulin Museum
activities.description: Museum documenting Paris at war.

activities.name: L'Amuse Bouche
activities.description: The cheese soufflés (soufflé au fromage) and Soufflé au Grand Marnier are highly recommended.

activities.name: L'Assiette
activities.description: Reservation recommended.

activities.name: Le Bis du Severo
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Bistot du Dôme
activities.description: Seafood.

activities.name: La Cagouille
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: La Cantine du Troquet
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: La Cerisaie
activities.description: Reservation recommended.

activities.name: La Coupole
activities.description: The famous Art Deco brasserie, convenient for a quick meal before or after a train journey to or from Montparnasse station.

activities.name: Le Dôme
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Le Duc
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Les Grillades de Buenos Aires
activities.description: Small and intimate Argentinian restaurant. Amazing steaks. Everything about the food is wonderful.

activities.name: Le Moulin Vert
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Le Zeyer
activities.description: Famous Art Deco-style brasserie.

activities.name: Grande Arche de la Defense
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Centre des nouvelles industries et technologies
activities.description: The largest unsupported concrete span enclosed space in the world. Houses shops and offices, ESSEC Business School campus and a Hilton hotel.

activities.name: Musée de La Défense
activities.description: The museum presents the history of the development of La Défense, with many plans and scale models.

activities.name: Notre-Dame de Pentecôte
activities.description: This unusual church building was erected in 2001 and designed by Franck Hammoutène.

activities.name: Forteresse du Mont-Valérien
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Tours Aillaud
activities.description: South-west from the Grande Arche you can find these tall cylindrical concrete residential towers, painted in various colours. Representing a 1970s urbanistic experiment, the 18 towers remain controversial and unique, and

activities.name: Parc André Malraux
activities.description: The expansive, calm park around a lake extends westwards behind the Tours Aillaud, and is to La Défense what Central Park is to Downtown Manhattan. You can take spectacular pictures of the skyline juxtaposed against the

activities.name: Nanterre Cathedral
activities.description: Built in the 1920s, it is no match for the Notre Dame de Paris in either historical importance or architectural detailing, but it can be an interesting destination for those interested in sacral architecture.

activities.name: Paris La Défense Arena
activities.description: A domed stadium near the Grande Arche. It opened in October 2017, and its main tenant is the rugby union club Racing 92. The facility can host everything from court sports to concerts, with configurations ranging from 10

activities.name: Pavillon des Indes
activities.description: The wooden Indian pavilion was moved from Paris to Courbevoie following the Universal Exhibition of 1878, and remains a curiosity amidst the much more modern architecture of the commune.

activities.name: Théâtre des Hauts-de-Seine
activities.description: A 19th-century theatre building.

activities.name: Public swimming pool and ice rink
activities.description: do in Paris

activities.name: Forêt domaniale de la Malmaison
activities.description: Little known forest, comparable with the Bois de Boulogne on the other side of the Seine

activities.name: Jardins de l'Eau Retrouvée
activities.description: Parc with fountains and large childrens playground

activities.name: Les 4 Temps
activities.description: The Quatre Temps shopping mall, which when constructed was the largest shopping centre in Europe, has numerous big name and small stores, several cinemas (showing French and subtitled foreign movies) as well as a large s

activities.name: Côté Parvis
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Quinte & Sens
activities.description: Under the direction of chef Jean-Paul Corbillet, the Quinte & Sens offers modern cuisine, including two-course "power lunches" for €29.

activities.name: Panthéon
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Jardin des Plantes

activities.name: Arènes de Lutèce

activities.name: Musée de l'Institut du Monde Arabe
activities.description: The building has a freely accessible rooftop which allows for a beautiful bird's-eye view of the northern half of Paris.

activities.name: Musée National Du Moyen Âge

activities.name: Eglise Saint-Étienne-du-Mont
activities.description: St. Geneviève was responsible for saving Paris from the Huns in 451 and her shrine in the church has been a popular place of pilgrimage ever since. The church as it stands dates from between 1492 and 1626 and is a mix of

activities.name: Eglise Saint-Julien-le-Pauvre
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Eglise Saint-Médard
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Eglise Saint Nicolas du Chardonnet
activities.description: A controversial church serving as de facto headquarters of the arch-traditionalist Society of Saint Pius X, who occupied the church in 1977 and have ignored subsequent eviction orders.

activities.name: Grande Mosquée de Paris
activities.description: Established in 1926, it was meant to show appreciation for Muslim help in fighting the Germans. The café serves excellent mint tea and North African food.
activities.location: 2 bis, place du puits de l'Ermite

activities.name: Église Saint-Jacques-du-Haut-Pas
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: l'Epée de Bois
activities.description: The name of the store and the street it's on both translate to the "wooden sword", and you find those here along with any number of wooden toys for children ages 1 to 10. They also have books and games.

activities.name: La Fontaine aux Vins
activities.description: One of several wine shops along the lower part of the street. The wines are of high quality, but barely more pricey than the ones you'll find in the grocery store. They offer tastings outside on the street, and delivery

activities.name: Gepetto & Vélos
activities.description: OK, so if you are just staying for a day or two it makes no sense whatever to buy a bike, but if you are going to be around for a while having your own might just feel better than renting one from the RATP. If so, you co

activities.name: Shakespeare and Co.
activities.description: Probably one of the most eccentric bookstores you will ever visit, this must-see was established in 1951 by George Whitman. Three rambling floors literally crammed with books, both new and (mainly) second-hand. The beddi

activities.name: Le Grenier de Notre Dame
activities.description: A vegetarian restaurant with a great selection including a number of vegan items, the Grenier is, as the name suggests, just around the corner from the cathedral. The English-speaking staff is super friendly.

activities.name: Jardin des Pâtes
activities.description: The range of pasta dishes is just fantastic at this cute little restaurant tucked away near the Jardin des Plantes. Although some meat dishes are served there is a huge range of choice for vegetarians as well.

activities.name: Ambiance De L'Inde
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Chez Ernest
activities.description: Very nice little restaurant in a small side-street. Typical and less typical French food.

activities.name: Kootchi
activities.description: Afghan cuisine, small and cosy, food is not too spicy, good value for money.

activities.name: Le Petit Prince de Paris
activities.description: A notably Parisian restaurant experience, but with friendly and warm service. Traditional French food (try the duck with feathers) and deliciously complicated sauces. Relaxed and uncrowded, but reserve in the morning for

activities.name: Le Volcan

activities.name: Bibimpap
activities.description: Korean restaurant.

activities.name: Coco de Mer
activities.description: Specialities from the Seychelles.

activities.name: Le Petit Pontoise

activities.name: La Tour d'Argent
activities.description: For those with fat wallets la Tour d'Argent is a must-do. One of the oldest restaurants in Paris, on the Seine, it is famous for its duck recipes.

activities.name: Cimetière du Père Lachaise
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Le Lion Indomptable
activities.description: A small place that serves excellent Cameroonian and Senegalese food. The chef/waiter is a character and will keep you entertained.

activities.name: Lou Pascalou
activities.description: Well hidden but worth seeking out, this unassuming restaurant serves happily inexpensive good food.

activities.name: Ô Chateau, Paris Wine Tasting
activities.description: Discover French wines with a native French sommelier. Informative, relaxing, and fun. In English in a beautiful Parisian loft.

activities.name: Antoine
activities.description: This shop sells stylish umbrellas and old-style canes, meant to last a lifetime. Also fancy hats, scarfs and gloves.

activities.name: Carrefour Express Paris Saint Honoré
activities.description: A small version of the French Carrefour supermarket chain has the appearance of a neighbourhood shop, with stalls filled with fresh fruit and vegetables on its shopfront, and is about the only place to get reasonably-pri

activities.name: Le Carrousel du Louvre
activities.description: A diverse underground shopping precinct adjoining the Louvre Museum. Open daily including Sundays. There is also a direct access into the Louvre.

activities.name: Forum les Halles
activities.description: In the late 1960s what was Paris's primary farmers' market moved out to the suburbs to be replaced by a park above ground, and a sprawling underground shopping centre below. The interior design is strikingly period (thin

activities.name: Jean-Paul Hévin
activities.description: The chocolatier is known for his excellent hand made chocolates especially the "praliné croquant".

activities.name: Librairie Galignani
activities.description: British and American bookshop, specialising in fine arts.

activities.name: Minuit Moins 7
activities.description: The "cordonnerie" repairs shoes and leather with the best materials.

activities.name: Place Vendôme
activities.description: High-end jewelry and watch boutiques on the place created by Napoleon I.

activities.name: Lorenz Bäumer
activities.description: On her wedding day Princess Charlène de Monaco wore a Lorenz Bäumer tiara. This new jewellery-maker fashions novel forms combined with classical influences.

activities.name: Van Cleef & Arpels
activities.description: Princess Caroline de Monaco got her wedding ornament at this traditional shop, open since 1906.

activities.name: Patek Philippe Salons
activities.description: The Swiss luxury watch manufacturer, founded in 1851, is known for precise mechanics.

activities.name: W.H. Smith
activities.description: The largest English-language bookshop in Paris carries many of the newest releases.

activities.description: Small, owner-operated bistro with traditional French country food in a nearly rustic setting. As such it's not exactly veggie-friendly.

activities.name: À la Cloche des Halles
activities.description: The bell no longer rings, but the brasserie-restaurant still has the flair from the old little bars from the good old times named "troquets".

activities.name: La Robe et le Palais
activities.description: Small restaurant serving mostly tasty Basque food. Fantastic choice of wines.

activities.name: Café Marly
activities.description: Part of the Grand Louvre redevelopment. Café Marly was opened in 1994 and is on the balcony on the northern terrace of the Cour Napoléon. Patrons can enjoy direct views of the Louvre Pyramid while sitting back in comfort
activities.location: 93, rue de Rivoli / cour Napoléon du Louvre

activities.name: Macéo
activities.description: What was once just a great wine bar with decent food has become a must-visit restaurant with the addition of star chef Thierry Bourbonnais. Second-empire atmosphere with fantastic food.

activities.name: Mystery Cuisine
activities.description: The restaurant serves fusion cuisine inspired by French Vietnamese meals in an atmosphere perfect for couples.

activities.name: Les Archives Nationales
activities.description: The Archives Nationales have one of the largest and most important archival collections in the world, a testimony to the very ancient nature of the French state which has been in existence for more than twelve centuries

activities.name: Musée Cognacq-Jay
activities.description: The Cognacq-Jay museum houses the collection of 18th-century art amassed by Ernest Cognacq, founder of La Samaritaine department stores, and his wife Marie-Louise Jaÿ.

activities.name: Musée des Arts et Métiers

activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Foundation Henri Cartier-Bresson
activities.description: The Foundation of Henri Cartier-Bresson is a private foundation for public use and for the memories and conservation of the photographer Henri Cartier Bresson and his wife Martine Franck. There are permanent displays and

activities.name: Institut Tessin
activities.description: Swedish cultural institute and museum

activities.name: Au Réparateur de Bicyclettes
activities.description: If you would rather have a funkier bike than the ones you can rent from the RATP you would do a lot worse than checking out this little bicycle repair shop which also offers a wide range of new and used bikes. Their spec

activities.name: Marché du Temple
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Camille
activities.description: Fantastic fresh French food. It is worth the wait.

activities.name: Chez Janou
activities.description: Chez Janou has such a pretty location that it's worth an evening visit just for that. But the food and atmosphere are also amazing. Vegetarians be warned, the tortellini is not the token veggie dish; there isn't one.

activities.name: Page 35
activities.description: The name apparently refers to some section of some newspaper or another apparently with social listings. The carte features fantastic crêpe opportunities making this a must for lunch or lingering over a carafe or two of

activities.name: Le Petit Marché
activities.description: This is one of those charming little Parisian bistros which is off on its own a little. The wait staff couldn't possibly be friendlier so this is a good place to bring people to show them how nice Parisian dining can be.

activities.name: Le Potager du Marais
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Jardin d'Acclimatation
activities.description: The northwest corner of the Bois de Boulogne is occupied by the oldest operating amusement park in the world, the Jardin d'Acclimatation, which is mainly known for offering a wide range of amusements which are appropriat

activities.name: Jardins du Trocadéro
activities.description: Tourists cross this park with the large fountain on the way to the Place du Trocadéro viewpoint to the Eiffel Tower.

activities.name: Villa La Roche
activities.description: see in Paris

activities.description: see in Paris

activities.name: Immeuble Molitor
activities.description: An 8-storey block of flats, built in 1932–1934.

activities.name: Castel Béranger
activities.description: Castel Béranger is an apartment building that was the first entirely art nouveau building in Paris, insulted at the time as "Castel Derangé". Its richly decorated facade won a city competition in 1898. It contains 36 apa

activities.name: Hôtel Guimard
activities.description: Guimard's own home, built in 1909-12. It is less decorated than Castel Béranger, but still beautifully shaped. Privately owned and not open to the public.

activities.name: Hôtel Mezzara
activities.description: House designed by Guimard in 1910-1 for Paul Mezzara, the owner and designer of a lace and embroidery manufacturer. As of October 2025, under renovation to become a museum dedicated to Guimard, expected to open in 2027-8

activities.name: Bois de Boulogne
activities.description: A park along the western edge of the 16ème arrondissement of Paris, near the suburb of Boulogne-Billancourt. The Bois de Boulogne has an area of {{km2

activities.name: Swim at Piscine Molitor
activities.description: A stunning classical swimming pool complex. The pool is known for its Art Deco designs and the popular introduction of the bikini by Louis Réard on 5 July 1946. The character Pi Patel in the novel/movie Life of Pi was na

activities.name: French Open
activities.description: The second of each year's four Grand Slam tennis tournaments is held over two weeks in late May and early June at Stade Roland Garros in the Bois de Boulogne. The venue is open for tours year-round whenever tournaments a

activities.name: Paris Saint-Germain
activities.description: PSG play soccer in the top-tier Ligue 1. They often win it and qualify for European tournaments. Parc des Princes stadium (capacity 48,000) is south side of Bois de Boulogne. The club name derives from St-Germain-en-Laye
activities.location: Parc des Princes, 24 Rue du Commandant Guilbaud

activities.name: Stade Français
activities.description: Rugby club in the Top 14, and a perennial title contender. Stade's traditional home ground of Stade Jean-Bouin, across the street from Parc des Princes, reopened in 2013 after a major renovation. Ticket information is av

activities.name: Horse racing
activities.description: Some of the world's best thoroughbred racing can be seen at two venues in the Bois de Boulogne.

activities.name: Hippodrome de Longchamp
activities.description: Used exclusively for flat racing, the venue hosts many of France's top races, including the internationally famous Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe on the first weekend in October.

activities.name: Hippodrome d'Auteuil
activities.description: Used exclusively for steeplechase racing, and hosts many top-quality races in that discipline. Its flagship event is the Grand Steeple-Chase de Paris.

activities.name: Rue de Passy
activities.description: Lined with high-end clothes and homewares shops

activities.name: Passy Plaza
activities.description: buy in Paris

activities.name: Centre Commercial des Belles Feuilles
activities.description: This shopping centre features a vast Casino supermarket and a number of smaller shops, and is very useful for self-caterers. Several other supermarkets are located nearby.

activities.name: Carette
activities.description: This Pâtisserie and Salon de thé also has great food. The salads are amazing, and there is delicious cakes for dessert.

activities.name: La Gare Chaussée De La Muette
activities.description: eat in Paris

activities.name: Le Malakoff
activities.description: Just take the menu of the day

activities.name: Le Coq
activities.description: A hip and trendy modern brasserie where the 16th arrondissement's posh population meets

activities.name: Café Brassac
activities.description: Very nice cafe with good lunch and dinner. Lots of people go their for business lunch

activities.name: L'Astrance
activities.description: 3 Michelin stars. Considered one of the best restaurants of Paris. Do not go there, if you are hungry and want to get full.

cultural.tip: Always greet with 'Bonjour' when entering any shop or restaurant.
Lunch is typically 12-2 PM. Many restaurants close between meals.
Parisians dress smartly. Avoid athleisure in nicer restaurants.
Try basic French phrases ('merci', 's'il vous plaît'). Locals genuinely appreciate the effort.
Watch for pickpockets on the Métro and at tourist sites. Keep valuables in front pockets.
